content: read reviews/595.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/595.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/595.md.

file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/595.lean`

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line (`import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`), immediately after the copyright block, separated by one blank line.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdÅ‘s Problem 595`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/595`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (Folkman and NeÅ¡etÅ™ilâ€“RÃ¶dl results on graphs with no Kâ‚„ that are not unions of triangle-free graphs).
- [x] Bibliography entries provided for `[Er87]`, `[Fo70]`, `[NeRo75]`.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos595 ... end Erdos595`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos595`).
- [x] No `open` / `open scoped` statements needed; none present. N/A.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- N/A â€” no helper `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_595` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions (snake_case for terms of Props).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references the source paper `[Er87]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks: *"Is there an infinite graph G which contains no Kâ‚„ and is not the union of countably many triangle-free graphs?"* â€” this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) â†” P` (Case 1 applied correctly).
- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the question as originally asked: existence of an infinite graph that is Kâ‚„-free and not expressible as a countable union of triangle-free subgraphs.
- [x] `answer(sorry)` is used (problem is open, answer unknown).
- [x] Quantifiers appear **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

**Formalization review:** The RHS uses `âˆƒ (V : Type) (_ : Infinite V) (G : SimpleGraph V)` to quantify over an infinite simple graph, `G.CliqueFree 4` for Kâ‚„-freeness, and `Â¬âˆƒ (H : â„• â†’ SimpleGraph V), (âˆ€ i, H i â‰¤ G) âˆ§ (âˆ€ i, (H i).CliqueFree 3) âˆ§ (âˆ€ u v, G.Adj u v â†’ âˆƒ i, (H i).Adj u v)` to express that G cannot be decomposed into countably many triangle-free subgraphs covering all edges. This is a faithful encoding: `â„•`-indexed families model "countably many," `H i â‰¤ G` ensures subgraph containment, `CliqueFree 3` is triangle-freeness, and the covering condition captures "union."

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research open` â€” a valid category.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present: `AMS 5`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single entry; trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Combinatorics).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjecture uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` form (acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in any definitions (no definitions present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A â€” no variants present. The file contains a single main theorem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`âˆ€`, `âˆƒ`, `âˆ§`, `Â¬`, `â†”`, `â†’`, `â„•`, `â‰¤`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` â€” not verified in this review (requires build environment).
- [ ] Custom linter compliance â€” not verified (requires build environment). Expected to pass based on manual inspection.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (all manually-verifiable items pass)

The file is clean and fully conformant with the checklist. The formalization faithfully captures the ErdÅ‘sâ€“Hajnal problem about the existence of an infinite Kâ‚„-free graph that cannot be decomposed into countably many triangle-free subgraphs. The `answer(sorry) â†” P` form is correctly used for this yes/no question. Documentation is thorough with three bibliography entries. No issues found.

content.text: The review passed (**Result: PASS**). No changes needed.
